meta.title: Florian Handke | Head of Industrial Security
meta.description: Head of Industrial Security at Campus Schwarzwald specializing in Applied Cryptography, PKI, Device Identity, and Industrial Cybersecurity.

header.name: Florian Handke
header.subtitle: Head of Industrial Security · Campus Schwarzwald · M.Sc. Mechanical Engineering
header.introduction: Head of Industrial Security at Campus Schwarzwald, a cluster of companies in the mechanical engineering and manufacturing industries. Holding a Master's degree (M.Sc.) in Mechanical Engineering with a specialization in Production Engineering, I am responsible for ensuring the security of industrial machinery and components. My expertise focuses on Public Key Infrastructure (PKI), digital identities, and their applications in industrial environments.

paragraphs: I combine engineering pragmatism with technical expertise in applied cryptography and industrial cybersecurity. With a background in mechanical engineering, I understand how the industry works. I then moved into software and security, gaining in-depth knowledge through hands-on experience.

I thrive on taking ownership, leading technical teams, speaking at conferences, teaching students and engineers, and building solutions that solve real-world problems. I love details, thinking end-to-end, and presenting complex content to different audiences. I specialize in turning complex security concepts into practical engineering solutions that balance technical feasibility with cost-effectiveness—because security is always a tradeoff.

industrialSecurity.title: Industrial Security
items: Expertise in IEEE 802.1 AR device identities (IDevID & LDevID)

featuredProject.title: Trustpoint – Open Source Trust Anchor
featuredProject.description: Co-created and technically led the development of Trustpoint, an open-source trust anchor and PKI platform developed together with contributors from multiple organizations. Trustpoint is a platform for managing digital machine identities and certificate lifecycles in industrial environments. It supports secure device onboarding, certificate issuance, renewal, revocation, and decommissioning using standardized protocols and can operate locally in segmented, offline, or air-gapped OT networks. By combining automation, user-friendly workflows, and vendor-independent standards, Trustpoint helps manufacturers, integrators, and operators reduce security risks and implement trustworthy industrial communication without requiring deep PKI expertise.

personalFacts: I live in a small village in the Black Forest, Baden-Württemberg, with my wife and two daughters.
I play football as a hobby.
Hands-on engineer who loves taking action and building things.
Every good concept starts with a sequence diagram.
Good user experience matters—even (especially!) in security software.
The world has lost a carpenter and an electrician in me—I'm an expert in building trade show exhibits.
I probably have the right adapter for your plug.
